sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Steel Dynamics: "It's an excellent company"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Steel Dynamics as an excellent company, praising its management, employee relations, and integrated operations. He views it as a well-run domestic steel producer, particularly in contrast to the broader perception of the steel industry.

feed.title: GE Aerospace Order Surge And Reorganization Shape Outlook To 2026
feed.summary: GE Aerospace reported a 74% surge in Q4 orders and double-digit revenue growth, resulting in a backlog nearing US$190 billion. The company is reorganizing by integrating its Technology & Operations team into the commercial business and making leadership changes to enhance efficiency and prepare for continued demand into 2026. This move aims to align product development, manufacturing, and customer-facing teams to better convert order momentum into sustainable cash generation.

feed.title: White House Rate Cap Proposal Tests American Express Profit Mix And Valuation
feed.summary: The White House has proposed capping U.S. credit card interest rates, a move that could significantly impact American Express's (NYSE:AXP) revenue model. This proposal directly affects their core revenue from annual percentage rates, potentially forcing adjustments in how the company balances interest income, fees, and merchant discount revenue. Investors are advised to monitor how American Express adapts its credit standards, rewards, and pricing strategies in response to this regulatory change.

feed.title: Assessing UDR (UDR) Valuation After Fresh Analyst Upgrades And Improved Multifamily Fundamentals
feed.summary: Recent analyst upgrades for UDR, driven by positive multifamily real estate fundamentals, have highlighted the apartment REIT to investors. Despite the stock trading at a P/E ratio significantly higher than its industry peers, analysis suggests it might be undervalued by about 6.5% with a fair value around $40.36, primarily due to strong occupancy and rent growth potential. However, risks like new supply and tighter rent regulation could impact future performance.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Expects Soft Industry Backdrop to Persist for Vail Resorts (MTN)
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Vail Resorts (MTN) to $150, maintaining an "Equal Weight" rating due to an expected soft industry backdrop in 2026, consistent with muted fundamentals seen in 2025. The company recently cut its 2026 earnings outlook after suffering from one of the weakest early snowfall periods in over 30 years in the western U.S., which negatively impacted visitation and spending. Despite Vail's shift towards a subscription-style model to mitigate weather-related swings, skier visits and lift revenue were down significantly compared to the previous year.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on 3M Company: “You Have My Blessing to Buy It Right Here”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has endorsed 3M Company (NYSE: MMM) as a buying opportunity, despite a negative market reaction to its recent earnings report. Cramer believes the market overreacted and that 3M is ahead of its medium-term financial targets, with a strong record of beating earnings estimates. He sees the company's "harsh pullback" as a chance to buy into a turnaround story at a discount.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Sees Rising Competitive Pressure Weighing on Conagra (CAG)
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Megan Alexander Clapp has lowered her price target for Conagra Brands (NYSE: CAG) to $18 from $19, maintaining an Equal Weight rating, due to increasing competitive pressure from value-driven pricing, promotions, and private labels expected to continue into 2026. The company is facing challenges like uneven demand, supply chain disruptions, higher ingredient costs, and potential long-term risks from changing consumer preferences and weight-loss drugs, leading to a significant drop in its stock value. Conagra's CEO has stated the company is prioritizing cash flow generation and debt reduction over acquisitions.
